mission: Lane TOOL-GRAPH: Scope cross-tool dependency graph implementation
verdict: NOT GREENFIELD. The tool system already has significant pipeline infrastructure spread across three layers: (1) typed-result.ts formal framework, (2) tool .txt description files with ad-hoc prose pipelines, (3) system prompts with sequential tool usage guidance. The graph codifies these existing informal patterns into a structured, queryable, single-source-of-truth format.

subagent_contributions.finding: 104 tools across 3 directories, BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS structure, cross-cutting patterns
Acyclic DAG, 15 external consumers, 6 Effect services, cross-tool import edges
95+ BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS, 15 conditional flags, dynamic description enrichment, no structured relationships, LLM sees only name+desc+schema
20+ documented pipeline patterns across typed-result.ts, .txt files, system prompts, and code. NOT greenfield — codifying existing ad-hoc patterns.

layer_1_typed_result_framework.description: Formal, structured pipeline framework — the graph's natural integration point

key_components.mechanism: Same-category heuristic (TOOL_CATEGORIES). Wrong for data-flow — suggests grep after smart_find instead of read_source.
On failure: retry self, fallback to diagnose tools. On denied: exclude self.
Structured {action, target, command} hints for the model.
Wraps every tool execution in the TypedResult pipeline — the PRODUCTION BOUNDARY.
Formats pipeline hints as human-readable text with [OK]/[FAILED] markers.
JSON marker embedded in output text — not currently parsed by any downstream code.

key_components.status: EXISTS BUT NEVER CALLED — structural parser for typed results is built but unused.

layer_2_tool_prose_documentation.description: Every tool's .txt file documents its pipelines in ad-hoc natural language. These are the graph's seed data.

discovered_patterns.text: 'you MUST use the Read tool first to read the file's contents. This tool will fail if you did not read the file first.'
'You must use your Read tool at least once in the conversation before editing.'
'If you are unsure of the correct file path, use the glob tool to look up filenames by glob pattern.'
'When you are doing an open-ended search... use the Task tool instead'
'DO NOT use it for file operations... use the specialized tools instead.'
'Prefer smart_bun, smart_git, smart_grep, smart_find, smart_sd, read_source...'
'If you want to read a specific file path, use the Read or Glob tool instead...'
'Agents use read_artifact(context) to get the latest curated view.'
'use read_artifact on the source_artifact path to pull the full context...'
'Use the Task tool to have explore agent process this file with Grep and Read...'
'Report a tool that isn't working as intended... aggregated into a feedback artifact.'
'The consolidator assembles all fragments for a file.'
'Produce the final session report by consuming ALL artifacts from the curated context.'
'The output includes a task_id you can reuse later to continue the same subagent session.'

layer_3_system_prompts.description: System prompts teach the LLM pipeline patterns at the meta level

patterns.text: 'If some tool calls depend on previous calls... do NOT call these tools in parallel and instead call them sequentially.'
'Use specialized tools instead of bash commands when possible... Read for reading instead of cat/head/tail, Edit for editing instead of sed/awk...'
'Use the result to take the next step (e.g. pick one match, read that file, then act); do not search again in a loop.' — explicit grep→read chain
'Parallelize tool calls whenever possible - especially file reads.'
'You must determine your next action based on the tool call results'
'When doing file search, prefer to use the Task tool in order to reduce context usage.'

what_exists: TOOL_CATEGORIES (7 categories, 18 tools — coarse but functional)
STRUCTURED_MARKER for output parsing (built but unused)
what_is_missing: Structured data-flow edges (fromTool output shape → toTool input shape)
Tool output shape classification (what kind of data does each tool produce?)
Tool input shape classification (what kind of data does each tool consume?)
Single-source-of-truth graph (edges scattered across prose, system prompts, and TypedResult)
Graph-aware tool filtering (replace flag-based gating with graph-based subsets)
Graph-wired suggestions (replace same-category heuristic with data-flow edges)
Automated documentation (current pipelines are hand-written prose)

seed_from_existing.maps_to: alternatives edges + category field on nodes
feeds edges with description + example fields
Pipeline guidance injected into model-facing descriptions
Node inventory (every ID gets a node)

ToolGraph.flag_gating: Record<string, string[]> — maps feature flag to gated tool IDs

OutputShape.kind: file_paths | file_contents | json | diff | coordination_claim | plan_id | checkpoint_id | finding_entry | validation_result | stdout | search_results | lsp_locations | context_snapshot | event_stream

InputShape.kind: file_path | glob_pattern | regex | command | session_id | plan_id | json_document | url | event_filter | invalidation_scope
InputShape.accepts_from: string[] (OutputShape.kind values that can feed this)

what_the_graph_enables.detail: Replace 20+ scattered prose patterns in .txt files + TypedResult + system prompts with one queryable graph. Tools auto-document their pipelines.
Replace same-category heuristic (typed-result.ts:245) with getDownstream(toolId). After smart_find → suggest read_source. After task → suggest task_board.
Wire fromOutput() (typed-result.ts:380, currently unused) to parse STRUCTURED_MARKER from tool output. Enable programmatic pipeline chaining.
Inject graph-derived 'After this tool, consider:' into each tool's description during registry.ts tools() resolution. Replaces manual prose in .txt files.
Graph's flag_gating map replaces hardcoded conditional inclusions in registry.ts:539-644.
Category-based filtering reduces 100+ tools to 15-25 context-relevant tools per agent phase.
BFS/DFS across produces→consumes edges. 'find and fix all type errors' → grep → read_source → smart_edit → bun.
Middleware reads downstream edges after each tool execution.
Graph traversal → auto-generated per-tool docs with 'Common pipelines', 'Feeds into', 'Fed by'.

phase_1_graph_types_and_data.action: Define tool-graph.ts (types) + tool-graph-data.ts (data). Populate from existing: TOOL_CATEGORIES → nodes+alternatives, .txt files → feeds edges, BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS → node inventory, registry flags → flag_gating.

phase_2_wire_typed_result.action: Replace same-category heuristic in suggestedToolsAfterSuccess (typed-result.ts:245) with graph.getDownstream(toolId). Wire fromOutput() (line 380) to actually parse STRUCTURED_MARKER.

phase_3_description_enrichment.action: Add describeGraphSuggestions(toolId) to registry.ts tools() method. Inject 'After this tool succeeds, consider: ...' into each tool's description. Replaces the need for hand-written prose in .txt files.

phase_4_flag_aware_subsets.action: Use graph.flag_gating in registry.ts builtin array instead of hardcoded conditionals.

phase_5_expose_service.action: Expose ToolGraph.Service Effect service. Add to app-runtime.ts.

smoking_guns.title: This is NOT greenfield — 20+ pipeline patterns already exist in prose, system prompts, and TypedResult
smoking_guns.detail: Every tool's .txt file already documents its pipelines in natural language. write.txt says 'use Read first', curate-context.txt says 'agents use read_artifact', truncate.ts says 'Use the Task tool'. System prompts teach sequential tool usage. TypedResult has suggestNextTools/nextAffordances. The graph codifies this scattered prose into a structured, queryable format.

smoking_guns.title: fromOutput() at typed-result.ts:380 exists but is never called — structured parsing is built but unused
smoking_guns.detail: The TypedResult framework embeds a STRUCTURED_MARKER in tool output and has a parser (fromOutput) to read it back. But nothing calls fromOutput(). Wiring this up would enable programmatic pipeline chaining — tool B parses tool A's output to auto-fill its parameters.

smoking_guns.title: TOOL_CATEGORIES covers only 18/104 tools — but the .txt files document pipelines for many more
smoking_guns.detail: TOOL_CATEGORIES has 7 categories with 18 tools. But every tool's .txt file documents at least one pipeline relationship. The graph can auto-seed from .txt file analysis (regex 'use X tool', 'see via Y', 'before calling Z') to cover far more than 18 tools.

smoking_guns.title: The description enrichment point already exists at registry.ts:694 — minimal change for maximum impact
smoking_guns.detail: describeTask() and describeSkill() already dynamically enrich tool descriptions during tools() resolution. Adding describeGraphSuggestions(toolId) here injects graph data into every tool's LLM-facing description with minimal code change and zero changes to Tool.Def.

smoking_guns.title: 15 conditional flags gate tool availability — graph must be the single-source-of-truth for this
smoking_guns.detail: The builtin array in registry.ts:539-644 has 15+ conditional inclusions. The graph's flag_gating map can replace these hardcoded conditionals, making tool subsets data-driven instead of code-driven.

dirty_files.assessment: No dirty files in the tool system, context/tools.ts, event/agent-queries.ts, or session/tools.ts. Safe greenfield.
